items.title: One Nation would become federal opposition if election held today, poll forecasts
items.summary: One Nation would win dozens of seats if a federal election was held today, according to a major opinion poll.

items.title: 'Stay with us': Dragons booed off the field at home ahead of fan day

items.title: Tax changes add to softening house sales caused by interest rate rises, agents say
items.summary: After riding a post-COVID wave of booming prices, real estate agents say SA's housing market is starting to level out, as federal budget tax changes and rising interest rates reduce buyer appetite.

items.title: Death toll reaches 82 after Chinese coal mine blast
items.summary: A gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China's Shanxi province sits at 82, state media reports. Two people remain missing.

items.summary: Collingwood's No.10 enjoys a 10-point win in his record-breaking match as the Magpies hold firm to down the Eagles at the MCG.

items.summary: Start-up founders say the federal government's CGT changes may see investment in new businesses go offshore. But Airtree Ventures co-founder Daniel Petre is warning against a scare campaign.

items.title: The perfect warm winter salad using crisp-roasted artichokes and grains - ABC News
items.summary: Crispy artichokes and textural grain make this salad a satisfying dinner and excellent next-day lunch.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX launches upgraded Starship
items.summary: Elon Musk's rocket company has launched its new Starship design to enable more frequent Starlink satellite launches and to send future NASA missions to the moon.

items.title: Vaccination 'not enough' to contain diphtheria spread in remote WA, disease official says
items.summary: The director of the Communicable Disease Control Directorate says vaccines help prevent more serious symptoms, but more efforts are needed to tackle the diphtheria outbreak in remote areas.

items.title: Qld man charged by Joint Counter Terrorism Team for alleged 'concerning posts'

items.title: Workforce shortages contribute to psychiatrist's lighter penalty after he sexually harassed nurse
items.summary: The only psychiatrist in a regional WA area is given a more lenient sanction for sexually harassing a nurse, partially due to a lack of other mental health specialists in the area.

items.title: Albany's abandoned WWII fuel tanks get new life as global graffiti gallery
items.summary: Tucked away in bushland on Western Australia's south coast, a hidden World War II site has become a sprawling canvas for street artists from all over the world.

items.title: Some of Australia's most isolated residents live with black mould and broken toilets in government housing

items.title: Health Minister denies health cuts, despite asking department to find $700m in cuts

items.title: A Collingwood champion's achievement has reopened an old wound outside Victoria

items.title: Indigenous artist Tony Albert's optimism is an act of generosity
items.summary: Girramay, Yidinji and Kuku-Yalanji artist Tony Albert hopes to "take every item of 'Aboriginalia' in Australia out of circulation".

items.title: WHO upgrades risk assessment for Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo to 'very high'

items.title: Residents concerned about future of The Palms development in Far North Queensland

items.title: Shares, start-ups, small businesses and ETFs: What Labor's capital gains tax changes mean in practice

items.title: Rural women share harrowing IVF stories at parliamentary inquiry

items.title: Willie Rioli Junior goes home to the Tiwis just when the islands' football club needs saving

items.title: ASIO assessment did not warn about heightened Hanukkah risks before Bondi attack

items.title: The budget's central weakness was revealed this week

items.title: Mosab Abu Toha documents life in Gaza under Israeli occupation in his poetry collection, Forest of Noise
items.summary: The month he turned 30, Mosab Abu Toha was blindfolded, beaten and detained by the Israel Defense Forces. The essay he wrote about it won a Pulitzer Prize.

items.summary: Australia's emerging agave industry is attracting interest from distillers, farmers and scientists who believe the spiky plant could transform everything from tequila production to sustainable aviation fuel.

items.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin has accused Ukraine of a "terrorist strike" on a student dormitory in the Russian-controlled Luhansk region, with a local official saying 10 were killed and 48 others injured.

items.title: Tax slug threatens clean energy 'flight' despite record federal support, says investor lobby
items.summary: As the federal government races towards its 2030 renewable energy goal, investors warn capital gains taxes changes risk upending the entire effort.

items.title: Tony Abbott returns to political life as Downer drops out of race for Liberal Party president
items.summary: Former prime minister Tony Abbott will be nominated unopposed to become the next federal president of the Liberal Party.

items.summary: The defence team for Daniel Billings has submitted evidence from a forensic psychiatrist that found he was in an "abnormal state of mind" when he killed his former partner.

items.summary: After leaving the al-Roj refugee camp on Thursday, a group of seven Australian women and 14 children has arrived in the Syrian capital, Damascus.

items.summary: The best friend of a Perth father killed by a shark off Rottnest Island says there was "absolutely no warning" before the attack.

items.title: Woman jailed over boat crash on River Murray at Mannum
items.summary: A woman who was likely more than three times the legal blood alcohol limit when she ran over a swimmer while driving a boat has been jailed for more than two years.

items.title: Tolu Koula to make Origin debut at stadium where his parents competed in Sydney Olympics

items.title: Staff exodus continues at Albury Wodonga Health as controversial development moves on
items.summary: The exodus of executives is continuing at embattled Albury Wodonga Health, with the board chair announcing he will not be seeking reappointment when his term ends.

items.title: Woman who died in house fire 'rejected' accused murderer, Victorian Supreme Court hears

items.title: China and Russia have again blocked moves to protect the endangered emperor penguin
